entries.position_summary: The country criticizes the United States for not ratifying the CTBT, dismissing congressional obstacles as an excuse. It claims Washington has taken no practical steps and is not interested in ratification.
entries.quote: 5 ratify the Treaty under invented pretexts. Since then, Washington has made no practical steps in this regard. We view the allegations that the Treaty cannot be passed through the Congress as a poor excuse. Consequently, we have concluded that the American establishment is not interested in ratifying the CTBT.

entries.position_summary: Russia reports near-completion of its CTBT International Monitoring System segment, with 31 of 32 planned facilities certified, signaling support for CTBT implementation and monitoring.
entries.quote: We are consistently pursuing a policy of completing the construction of facilities of the Russian segment of the International Monitoring System under the CTBT: the total number of certified facilities has reached 31 of the 32 planned.

entries.position_summary: The delegation criticizes the United States for failing to ratify the CTBT since 1999, dismisses U.S. explanations as excuses, and concludes that Washington has no interest in ratifying the treaty.
entries.quote: 5 Mr. Chair, We would like to highlight the situation around the Comprehensive Nuclear- Test-Ban Treaty (CTBT). As far back as 1999, the United States Congress denied to ratify the Treaty under an invented pretext. Since then, Washington has made no practical steps in this regard. We view the allegations that the Treaty cannot be passed through the Congress as a poor excuse. Consequently, we have concluded that the American establishment is not interested in ratifying the CTBT.

entries.position_summary: The country regrets that the CTBT has still not entered into force more than 25 years after opening for signature and notes that eight Annex 2 states have yet to ratify. It implicitly urges those remaining states to complete ratification.
entries.quote: We regret to note that over a quarter of a century since the CTBT was opened for signature, it has yet to enter into force. Despite intensive international efforts, eight CTBT Annex 2 States have not completed ratification procedures.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its support for and compliance with the CTBT, noting ratification in 2000 and strict fulfillment of obligations. It also emphasizes maintaining a voluntary moratorium on nuclear testing since the early 1990s.
entries.quote: Russia actively participated in drafting the CTBT, ratified it in 2000 and has strictly fulfilled its obligations under it. Since the early 1990s, our country has rigorously observed a voluntary moratorium on nuclear testing.

entries.position_summary: Russia justifies its withdrawal of CTBT ratification by citing long-standing U.S. failure to ratify the Treaty and what it calls Washington’s destructive stance. It criticizes the U.S. Congress for rejecting ratification in 1999 and alleges no subsequent practical steps by the United States toward ratification.
entries.quote: Madam Chair, Russia's withdrawal of its ratification of the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test- Ban Treaty (CTBT) came as a response to the disparity of approaches to the Treaty that had been incited by Washington's destructive stance for many years. As far back as 1999, the United States Congress denied to ratify the Treaty under invented pretexts. Since then, Washington has made no practical steps in this regard.

entries.position_summary: Russia reaffirms commitment to the CTBT, stating it remains a signatory and has completed its national segment of the International Monitoring System in late 2023 to support the Treaty's verification regime.
entries.quote: Russia remains a CTBT signatory state. Russia is still committed to the CTBT, which it demonstrated in late 2023 having completed the national segment of the International Monitoring System, a key component of the Treaty's verification mechanism.

entries.position_summary: The country explains its withdrawal of CTBT ratification as a response to asymmetry with the United States but affirms continued commitment to the Treaty’s purposes. It highlights completion of its national IMS segment and signals willingness to re-ratify once the United States ratifies.
entries.quote: Russia's withdrawal of its ratification of the Treaty has become a natural response to the disparity in obligations with the United States. Nevertheless, we remain committed to the purposes and objectives of the CTBT and have recently completed our national segment of the International Monitoring System, the largest certified segment to date. We stand ready to return to the issue of the CTBT ratification as soon as the United States ratifies it.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that the CTBT’s fate hinges on the political will of the eight remaining Annex 2 states and criticizes the United States for refusing to ratify and failing to take concrete steps toward ratification despite rhetorical signals. It implicitly urges progress toward entry into force by these states.
entries.quote: The fate of this most important international agreement remains uncertain and depends on the political will of the leadership of the eight States on the "List of 44" in Annex 2 to the CTBT that have not yet acceded to the Treaty. In this context, we should mention the destructive position of the United States, which, having officially refused to ratify the CTBT in 2018, has not taken any real steps to remedy the situation, despite the declarative statements by the current Washington administration about its desire to reconsider this stance.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns non-CTBT-ratifying states for making CTBT-related accusations against states that have ratified and are complying in good faith, implying non-ratifiers lack standing to do so. It affirms support for CTBT compliance by ratifying states.
entries.quote: It is particularly unacceptable when a State that has not ratified the CTBT allows itself to make accusations related to the Treaty against States that have ratified it and are complying with it in good faith.
